data.title: High Roller Technologies appoints Kristofer Muscat as COO
data.description:
• High Roller Technologies (NYSE:ROLR) announced the appointment of Kristofer Muscat as its new Chief Operating Officer, effective January 5th, 2025.
  • In this role, Muscat will oversee operational strategy, drive growth initiatives, and enhance High Roller’s commitment to delivering experiences in the online gambling and iCasino gaming industry.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: P&C insurer stocks slide on wildfire exposure, led by Mercury General
data.description:
A bunch of property & casualty insurance stocks tumbled in Friday premarket trading as they're poised to see billions of dollars of claims from the Los Angeles wildfires that have scorched thousands of acres in one of the U.S.'s densest and most affluent regions.

Shares in Los Angeles-based Mercury General (NYSE:MCY) plunged 39%, Allstate (NYSE:ALL) dropped 5.2%, Travelers (NYSE:TRV) fell 4.2%, AIG (NYSE:AIG) sank 3.4%, Progressive (NYSE:PGR) dipped 1.9%, and Hartford Financial Services (NYSE:HIG) declined 1.7%.

In a broader measure, the SPDR S&P Insurance ETF (KIE) slid 1.7% premarket.

The stock reactions come as the estimates of damage climb with the destruction of the largely uncontained fires. Accuweather boosted its estimate of total damages to $135B-$150B from $52B-$57B just a day earlier. On Thursday, J.P. Morgan doubled its estimate for insured losses in southern California to over $20B, from its earlier estimate of approaching $10B.

data.title: Tilray slips after Q2 FY25 revenue miss
data.description:
Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) traded ~8% lower in the premarket on Friday after the Canadian cannabis company fell short of Street forecasts for its Q2 FY25 revenue, despite exceeding expectations with its bottom line and FY25 outlook.

While its quarterly revenue rose ~9% YoY to $211M, driven by TLRY's beverage business, which added $63M with ~36% YoY growth, its cannabis and distribution segments underperformed, bringing $66M and $68M to the topline, respectively implying ~ a 2% YoY drop and ~2% growth.

However, the Ontario-based company reaffirmed its net revenue guidance of $950M—$1B for fiscal 2025, ahead of consensus estimates of $900M revenue.

"In our fiscal second quarter, Tilray achieved strong results while making significant progress on our strategic plan," CEO Irwin Simon said, adding, "As we enter the second half of the year, we remain committed to delivering on our financial guidance and driving shareholder value."

Meanwhile, the company's adjusted net loss reached $2M in Q2 from $3M in the prior year period as its adjusted gross profit margin improved to 30% from 27%, driven by the beverage division, where adj. gross margin reached 42% from 38%, respectively.

However, TLRY's adj. EBITDA for the quarter fell ~10% YoY to $9M, after a $1.8M impact attributed to the beverage segment's SKU rationalization.

Notably, the company announced a synergy program named Project 420 to achieve its $25M synergy plan through initiatives such as portfolio and operational optimization and cost savings.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: DAL, WBA and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were slightly lower on Friday, ahead of the highly anticipated December 2024 nonfarm payrolls report. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday:

  • Shares of Delta Air Lines (DAL) gained about 9% in pre-market trading. The largest U.S. carrier delivered a FQ4 2024 top- and bottom-line beat, helped by a booming holiday season and strong travel demand. "During the months of November and December, Delta (DAL) saw four of the top ten revenue days in the company's history and double-digit growth in cash bookings driven by both leisure and corporate travelers," the company said. Delta (DAL) also provided strong FQ1 2025 guidance. See how DAL performed in charts.
  • Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) stock surged more than 15% ahead of the opening bell. The operator of the second-largest U.S. pharmacy chain delivered a FQ1 2025 adjusted profit beat on a solid 7.5% Y/Y increase in sales. Walgreens (WBA) stock plunged 64.3% in a tough 2024, and also lots its place among the blue-chip Dow 30 firms, as the company undertook numerous store closures to improve profits. "While our turnaround will take time, our early progress reinforces our belief in a sustainable, retail pharmacy-led operating model," top boss Tim Wentworth said.
  • Constellation Brands (STZ) stock slipped more than 3% ahead of market open, after the Corona beer giant missed FQ3 2025 top- and bottom-line expectations. The company also reduced its fiscal year 2025 reported profit per share guidance and its organic net sales growth outlook. Chief executive Bill Newlands noted that Constellation (STZ) continued to "face the subdued spend and value seeking behaviors that emerged among legal drinking age consumers in Q2." He sees "near-term uncertainty on when consumers will revert to more normalized spending."
  • U.S.-listed shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) added about 1% in pre-market trading. The world's largest contract chipmaker announced a nearly 60% Y/Y increase in December 2024 revenue to NT$278.16B ($8.44B), amid red-hot demand for processors to power artificial intelligence applications. Taiwan Semi (TSM) - which makes chips for some of the biggest tech companies in the world including Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA) - said for January through December 2024 came in at NT$2.89T ($87.7B), an increase of 33.9%.

data.title: Bank of Montreal enters into automatic securities purchase plan
data.description:
Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO) has entered into an automatic securities purchase plan to facilitate repurchases of its common shares pursuant to its planned normal course issuer bid.

Under the bid, the bank intends to purchase for cancellation up to 20 million of common shares.

The purchase of any shares under the automatic securities purchase plan will not begin until after the approval of such normal course issuer bid by the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Canada and the Toronto Stock Exchange.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Pulse Biosciences announces CEO appointment
data.description:
• Pulse Biosciences (NASDAQ:PLSE) announced on Friday that Co-Chairman Paul Laviolette has been appointed to the additional post of CEO.
  • PLSE shares were trading -2.33% pre-market at $18.00.

data.title: Atai Life Sciences says Srinivas Rao assumes role of sole CEO
data.description:
• Atai Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATAI) on Friday announced that Srinivas Rao has assumed the role of sole Chief Executive Officer.
  • The company also promoted Kevin Craig to Chief Medical Officer, Glenn Short to Chief Scientific Officer and the appointment of Gerd Kochendoerfer as Chief Operating Officer.
  • As announced in May 2024, Co-Founder Srinivas Rao, was promoted to Co-CEO effective June 1, 2024, and assumed the role of CEO on January 1, 2025. Rao previously held the titles of Chief Scientific, Medical, and Executive Officer at companies ranging from venture-backed startups to vertically integrated, publicly traded pharmaceutical companies.
  • ATAI +1.23% premarket to $1.64.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IonQ CEO says 2024 at 'high end' of bookings, predicts $1B in revenue by 2030
data.description:
IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) Chairman and CEO Peter Chapman issued an update on Friday and said he expects bookings for the quantum computing company to be at the "high end" of its forecast, and forecast revenue could approach $1B by 2030.

Shares rose 7% in premarket trading, while other quantum stocks, such as Rigetti Computing (RGTI), Quantum Computing (QUBT) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) were also higher.

"We anticipate that 2024 results will be at the high end of our bookings and revenue guidance and are extremely excited about 2025," Chapman said in a statement. "We believe that IonQ will be profitable, with sales approaching $1 billion, by 2030."

Separately, on Friday, D-Wave said 2024 bookings would rise approximately 120% year-over-year over 2023 levels.

Quantum update

Chapman also gave an update on the state of quantum computing itself, after Nvidia (NVDA) Chief Executive Jensen Huang said earlier this week that "very useful" quantum computers are around 20 years away and sent stocks in the industry tumbling.

"One of the areas facing the most significant potential disruption is strong AI, where we believe natively quantum AI will outperform classical AI," Chapman explained. "Prudent leaders invest in things that have the potential for near-term returns."

By the end of 2023, investment in quantum had reached $50B, Chapman added, citing data provided by consulting firm McKinsey.

He also noted that companies like Nvidia, as well as Amazon, Google, IBM and Microsoft are already investing and hiring in quantum computing.

data.title: Immunocore reports cash, equivalents of $820M as of December 31
data.description:
• Immunocore (NASDAQ:IMCR) said its preliminary unaudited cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were ~$820 million as of December 31.
  • The firm said in Q4, it prepaid in full the loan outstanding under the Pharmakon loan agreement and also paid sales-related rebate accruals.

data.title: DataMetrex receives new P.O. for $440K
data.description:
• Datametrex AI (OTCPK:DTMXF) on Friday said its wholly owned subsidiary, Datametrex Korea, received a new Purchase Order (P.O.) of about $440K with projected gross profit of about 33%, on December 30, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release
